terms.definition: The SI unit of electrical resistance. One ohm allows one ampere to flow when one volt is applied.
The reciprocal of resistance, measured in siemens (S). G = 1/R.
The allowable deviation from the stated resistance value, expressed as a percentage.
The rate at which resistance changes with temperature, expressed in ppm/°C.
Operating a resistor below its maximum rated power to improve reliability and lifespan.
The fundamental law stating that voltage equals current times resistance (V = IR).
